_comment: Team-wide settings for the Claude Team — committed defaults for company agents. Permissions: broad allow for safe development workflows; explicit deny for production pushes + destructive operations to protect against accidental progress loss. User directive 2026-05-06: 'expand permissions for company agents — not direct production pushes anywhere, not full deletes/drops.' Personal overrides live in settings.local.json (gitignored).

SessionStart._purpose: Two-hook SessionStart chain. (1) session-start-brief.py: orchestrator briefing on every session start (startup/resume/clear/compact); auto-detects Tier 1 vs Tier 2 workspace shape; encodes the TapAgents thesis so the routing rule survives compactions + resumes. (2) session-tracking-register.py: auto-registers a stub entry in active-sessions.md keyed by Claude Code's payload.session_id (BL-055 v0.15.0); paired with the PreToolUse hook session-tracking-files.py and Stop hook session-tracking-seal.py; graduates protocols/session-coordination-protocol.md §247 from Future-enforcement to Current.

UserPromptSubmit._purpose: Two-stage UserPromptSubmit chain. (1) prompt-router.py: lightweight intent classifier — nudge toward subagent dispatch on code-intent verbs, nudge toward /park on side-thought patterns, silent on status / slash-command / acknowledgement prompts. Emits one `classify` telemetry event per turn (BL-035 v0.11.0). (2) prompt-router-feedback.py: classifier-feedback loop — reads the previous turn's classify event and emits `nudge_ignored` telemetry if the orchestrator clearly drove past the nudge. Order matters: prompt-router emits the current-turn classify first, then prompt-router-feedback evaluates the previous turn's classify against the current prompt.

Stop._purpose: Four-stage Stop hook chain. (1) stop-critic-check.py: blocks Stop if any project is mid-flight (blocked_on set, contested artifacts, unresolved BLOCKING critic concerns). (2) stop-dispatch-monitor.py: observational — reads .claude/workspace/_global/events.jsonl, counts orchestrator-source dispatch-gate blocks for this session, writes a memory pattern note + appends to MEMORY.md when ≥ 3. (3) stop-phase-transition.py: observational — M-D track slice S1 (telemetry-events.md §2.4). Diffs each project's state.json.current_phase against a sidecar snapshot at <workspace>/_global/.phase-snapshot.json and emits a state-machine/transition/<from>-<to> event per genuine phase change (local emit_event + cloud emit_event_http). Silent on first-seen bootstrap + no-change. Never blocks Stop. Dispatch-outcome (subagent-dispatch/outcome) is NOT in this hook — scoped to follow-up slice S1b (no reliable non-heuristic Stop-payload signal). (4) session-tracking-seal.py: BL-055 v0.15.0; reads the per-session sidecar at <workspace>/_global/sessions/<cc_session_id>.json, compares files_in_flight against git log main since started, and writes status: sealed | partial | noop into active-sessions.md. Never blocks Stop (stop-critic-check is the gate); bookkeeping only.

PreToolUse._purpose: Six-layer gate. (1) pre-tool-gate.py: classic safety — block direct edits to .env, seed.md, [CONTESTED] artifacts, dangerous bash patterns. (2) version-gate.py: framework version-bump discipline. (3) orchestrator-dispatch-gate.py: enforces orchestrator-dispatches-doesn't-implement — hard-blocks Edit/Write/NotebookEdit and mutating-Bash on the main thread (subagents bypass via agent_id/agent_type payload). (4) sync-discipline-gate.py: enforces protocols/sync-tapagents-protocol.md — blocks framework-sync commits/pushes on dev/main; canonical destination is sync-tapagents branch. Bypasses via `[sync-protocol-override: <reason>]` token in commit message. Added 2026-05-14 in response to v0.20.0 rider-commit incident. (5) framework-private-data-gate.py: BLOCKS a Write/Edit/NotebookEdit that would write a real private identifier (project slug, Neon endpoint, prod host, operator home path, repo path, or credential shape) into a framework file that propagates to the public mirror. Reuses the genericizer map (scripts/sync-src/manifest.json5 → genericize) + a secret-pattern subset as the source of truth; protected names (@tapintomymind/tap-agents, the brand-domain protect entry) + excluded files (memory/*, workspace/<slug>/*) + the sync-src self-skip set + genericize_exemptions are never blocked; fail-open. Added 2026-06-02 in response to the sync-genericizer remediation (PR#13 re-leak class). The authoring-time complement to the sync-time bare-codename lint + verify-genericize gate. (6) session-tracking-files.py: BL-055 v0.15.0; observational, NEVER blocks. Order: safety first, version-gate second, routing-discipline third, sync-discipline fourth, private-data fifth, manifest-tracking last.

PostToolUse._purpose: Single-hook PostToolUse chain. permission-denial-capture.py: observational, NEVER blocks. Captures tool calls whose response indicates a permission denial (Claude Code permission mode, settings.json deny rules, framework hook blocks) and emits a harness-block telemetry event to events.jsonl. Closes the coverage gap surfaced by 2026-05-12 BL-060 dispatch arc (see workspace/_global/org-designer-proposals/2026-05-12-curator-vocab-harness.md §2.5).

PostToolUseFailure._purpose: Single-hook PostToolUseFailure chain. permission-denial-capture.py: same script as PostToolUse, observational only. PRIMARY capture surface for framework-hook firings (PreToolUse exit-2 from orchestrator-dispatch-gate.py / pre-tool-gate.py / version-gate.py routes through PostToolUseFailure with stderr containing 'BLOCKED:' / 'TAPAGENTS_DISPATCH_GATE_FIRED_V1'). Without this event, framework gate firings are NOT captured by telemetry — the PreToolUse hooks emit their OWN block event, but for any non-framework denial (Claude Code permission mode, harness-classifier misfire, settings.json deny rule), this is the only capture point.

PermissionDenied._purpose: Single-hook PermissionDenied chain. permission-denial-capture.py: same script, observational only. Fires when Claude Code auto-mode classifier denies a tool call (separate code path from PostToolUseFailure). Hook input includes `reason` field with classifier's verbatim explanation. Coverage rationale: auto-mode denials are NOT routed through PostToolUseFailure; they go through this dedicated event per Claude Code hook spec (https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md). Hook does NOT return retry:true — denials stand as the classifier intended; we only capture for telemetry.

permissions._doc_allow: ALLOW: safe operations company agents perform routinely. Reads, builds, tests, lints, local + dev DB migrations, dev/feature-branch git pushes, package management. Deny rules below override these for destructive cases.
permissions._doc_deny: DENY: production-WRITING operations + destructive deletes/drops. Tier C per autonomous-ops protocol — explicit user authorization each time. Override allow rules above. PROD READS NOT DENIED: per user directive 2026-05-06 ('Prod read should never be blocked. Only the write or update or delete is what we are looking out for') — drizzle-kit introspect, psql -c "SELECT...", neonctl connection-string production, vercel logs/inspect/env-ls etc. all flow through. Only WRITE-shape commands targeting prod connection-strings are denied below: drizzle-kit push (schema mutation), drizzle-kit drop, psql DROP/TRUNCATE/DELETE FROM... WHERE 1=1, vercel remove/rm/project rm, neonctl *delete*. NOTE: 'main' is intentionally NOT denied for git push here — this settings.json governs the TapAgents framework repo, which is main-only (no QA branch). User directive 2026-05-06: 'TapAgents can be pushed to main since we are always iterating it.' Other repos with prod gating inherit their own settings.json with main:* denied per `project_branch_strategy.md`.
